[package]
name = "gleam-core"
version = "1.15.1"
authors = ["Louis Pilfold <louis@lpil.uk>"]
edition = "2024"
license = "Apache-2.0"

[dependencies]
# Error message and warning formatting
codespan-reporting = "0"
# Graph data structures
petgraph = "0.8"
# Template rendering
askama = "0"
# Markdown parsing
pulldown-cmark = { version = "0", default-features = false, features = [
  "html",
] }
# XDG directory locations
dirs-next = "2"
# SPDX license parsing
spdx = "0"
# Binary format de-serialization
bincode = { version = "2", features = ["alloc", "serde"] }
# cross platform single glob and glob set matching
globset = { version = "0", features = ["serde1"] }
# Checksums
xxhash-rust = { version = "0", features = ["xxh3"] }
# Pubgrub dependency resolution algorithm
pubgrub = "0.3"
# Used for converting absolute path to relative path
pathdiff = { version = "0", features = ["camino"] }
# Memory arena using ids rather than references

31	)]
32	pub struct Implementations {
33	    /// Whether the function has a pure-gleam implementation.
34	    ///
35	    /// It's important to notice that, even if all individual targets are
36	    /// supported, it would not be the same as being pure Gleam.
37	    /// Imagine this scenario:
38	    ///
39	    /// ```gleam
40	    /// @external(javascript, "wibble", "wobble")
41	    /// @external(erlang, "wibble", "wobble")
42	    /// pub fn func() -> Int
43	    /// ```
44	    ///
45	    /// `func` supports all _current_ Gleam targets; however, if a new target
46	    /// is added - say a WASM target - `func` wouldn't support it! On the other
47	    /// hand, a pure Gleam function will support all future targets.
48	    pub gleam: bool,
49	    pub can_run_on_erlang: bool,
50	    pub can_run_on_javascript: bool,
51	    /// Whether the function has an implementation that uses external erlang
52	    /// code.
53	    pub uses_erlang_externals: bool,
54	    /// Whether the function has an implementation that uses external javascript
55	    /// code.
56	    pub uses_javascript_externals: bool,
57	}
58	
59	impl Implementations {
60	    pub fn supporting_all() -> Self {
61	        Self {
62	            gleam: true,
63	            can_run_on_erlang: true,
64	            can_run_on_javascript: true,
65	            uses_javascript_externals: false,
66	            uses_erlang_externals: false,
67	        }
68	    }
69	}
70	
71	/// The purity of a function.
72	///
73	/// This is not actually proper purity tracking, rather an approximation, which
74	/// is good enough for the purpose it is currently used for: warning for unused
75	/// pure functions. The current system contains some false negatives, i.e. some
76	/// cases where it will fail to emit a warning when it probably should.
77	///
78	/// If we wanted to properly track function side effects - say to perform
79	/// optimisations on pure Gleam code - we would probably need to lift that
80	/// tracking into the type system, the same way that variant inference currently
81	/// works. This would require quite a lot of work and doesn't seem a worthwhile
82	/// amount of effort for a single warning message, where a much simpler solution
83	/// is generally going to be good enough.
84	///
85	/// In the future we may want to implement a full side effect tracking system;
86	/// this current implementation will not be sufficient for anything beyond a
87	/// warning message to help people out in certain cases.
88	///
89	#[derive(Debug, Clone, Copy, PartialEq, Eq, serde::Serialize, serde::Deserialize)]
90	pub enum Purity {
91	    /// The function is in pure Gleam, and does not reference any language
92	    /// feature that can cause side effects, such as `panic`, `assert` or `echo`.
93	    /// It also does not call any impure functions.
94	    Pure,
95	    /// This function is part of the standard library, or an otherwise trusted
96	    /// source, and though it might use FFI, we can trust that the FFI function
97	    /// will not cause any side effects.
98	    TrustedPure,
99	    /// This function is impure because it either uses FFI, panics, uses `echo`,
100	    /// or calls another impure function.
101	    Impure,
102	    /// We don't know the purity of this function. This highlights the main issue
103	    /// with the current purity tracking system. In the following code for example:
104	    ///
105	    /// ```gleam
106	    /// let f = function.identity
107	    ///
108	    /// f(10)
109	    /// ```
110	    ///
111	    /// Since purity is not currently part of the type system, when analysing the
112	    /// call of the local `f` function, we now have no information about the
113	    /// purity of it, and therefore cannot infer the consequences of calling it.
114	    ///
115	    /// If there was a `purity` or `side_effects` field in the `Type::Fn` variant,
116	    /// we would be able to properly infer it.
117	    ///
118	    Unknown,
119	}

content.text:
Implemented the fix in `compiler-core/src/type_/expression.rs`.

What changed:
- Record updates now accept record constructors with no `field_map`.
- For positional-only constructors, the compiler now creates an empty `FieldMap` with the constructor arity, allowing positional fields to be copied during `Constructor(..record)` updates.
- `RecordUpdateVariant` now owns its `FieldMap` so it can use either the constructor’s labelled-field map or a synthesized positional-only map.

Validation:
- Added a temporary regression test for:

```gleam
pub type Positional {
  Positional(Int, Int)
}

pub fn identity(positional: Positional) {
  Positional(..positional)
}
```

- Confirmed it failed before the fix with `RecordUpdateInvalidConstructor`.
- Confirmed it passed after the fix.
- Removed the temporary test as requested.
- Ran targeted existing record update tests successfully:

```text
cargo test -p gleam-core type_::tests::record_update -- --nocapture
```

Result: 11 passed.

Note: Final diff only includes the intended source fix in `compiler-core/src/type_/expression.rs`; the temporary test file diff was removed. Current working tree also shows unrelated existing changes in `Cargo.lock`, `hexpm/src/lib.rs`, and `reports/`, which I did not modify for this fix.
